name: bert-schemas

metadata.name: bert-schemas
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Bert Schemas

[![License: Apache](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) [![Twitter](https://img.shields.io/twitter/url/https/twitter.com/Infleqtion.svg?style=social&label=Follow%20%40Infleqtion)](https://twitter.com/Infleqtion)

## 🚀 Quick Install

```python
pip install bert-schemas
```

## 🧭 Introduction

These Pydantic schemas are used by projects such as Oqtant for defining and validating REST payloads.

modules.name: bert_schemas.projected
documentation:
A module that captures the features, and limitations, of optical objects
implemented by the Oqtant hardware projection system.
Copyright 2024 Infleqtion
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

functions.name: bert_schemas.projected.get_potential_from_weights
documentation:
Method to calculate projected potential based on given weights (intensities)
applied to each projected spot

Args:
    weights: height of each Gaussian spot

Returns:
    list[float]: Calculated total optical potential at the given positions

functions.name: bert_schemas.projected.get_corrected_projection_weights
documentation:
Method to calculate weights for each horizontal "spot" projected onto the atom ensemble to
attempt to achieve the passed optical object's "ideal" potential energy profile.
Implements first-order corrections for anamolous contributions from nearby spots,
inter-integer barrier centers, etc

Args:
    get_ideal_potential (Callable[[float], list]): Method for the optical object or any class
        that supports optical objects that calculates the specified "ideal" or "requested"
        potential energy profile
    time (float, optional): Time at which to correct

Returns:
    np.ndarray[float]: Calculated (optical intensity) contribution for each projected spot
        (diffraction frequency) used by the projection systems

functions.name: bert_schemas.projected.get_actual_potential
documentation:
Method to calculate the "actual" potential energy vs position for optical
objects/fields as realized by the Oqtant projection system. Includes effects,
and first-order corrections for, finite time updates and finite optical
resolution/optical objects being projected as sums of gaussians and energetic
clipping of optical potentials

Args:
    get_ideal_potential (Callable[[float], list]): Object method for request/ideal potential
    time (float, optional): Time to evaluate ideal potential
    positions (list[float], optional): Positions to evaluate the actual potential at

Returns:
    list[float]: Expected actual potential energy at the request positions

functions.name: bert_schemas.projected.gaussian
documentation:
Method that evaluates a standard gaussian form over the given input points

Args:
    xs (numpy.ndarray): Positions where the gaussian should be evaluated
    amp (float, optional): Gaussian amplitude
    center (float, optional): Gaussian center
    sigma (float, optional): Gaussian width
    offset (float, optional): Gaussian dc offset

Returns:
    np.ndarray: Gaussian function evaluated over the input points

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.projected.Settings.resolution
documentation: 1/e^2 diameter of projection system, microns

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.projected.Settings.position_step
documentation: grid step between projected spots, microns

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.projected.Settings.min_position
documentation: minimum position of projected light, microns

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.projected.Settings.max_position
documentation: maximum position of projected light, microns

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.projected.Settings.update_period
documentation: milliseconds between updates of projected light

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.projected.Settings.max_energy
documentation: maximum projected energy shift at any position, kHz

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.projected.Settings.terminator_width
documentation: 1/e^2 width of the terminator beam in um

classes.name: bert_schemas.questionnaire.UseReason
documentation:
# TECH_USE = "Technology Development"
# SCI_USE = "Scientific Research"
# EDU_USE = "Education"
# CUR_USE = "Curious"
# OTH_USE = "Other"

classes.name: bert_schemas.questionnaire.SchoolLevel
documentation:
# PRIMARY = "Primary (K-8)"
# SECONDARY = "Secondary (9-12)"
# COLLEGE = "College/University"
# POSTGRAD = "Post-Graduate"
# NOTGIVEN = "Not Given"
PRIMARY = "PRIMARY"

functions.name: bert_schemas.timing.ceiling
documentation:
Performs ceiling function with caller-specified precision

Args:
    x (float): _description_
    precision (int, optional): Number of decimal points. Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float: output

functions.name: bert_schemas.timing.floor
documentation:
Performs ceiling function with caller-specified precision

Args:
    x (float): _description_
    precision (int, optional): Number of decimal points. Defaults to 0.

Returns:
    float: output

functions.name: bert_schemas.timing.resolve_time
documentation:
Resolves input time to nearest 0.1 ms, corresponding to the allowed time resolution.

Args:
    t (float): input time in ms

Returns:
    float: output time in ms

functions.name: bert_schemas.timing.resolve_times
documentation:
Resolves input times to nearest 0.1 ms, corresponding to the allowed time resolution.

Args:
    ts (list): input time in ms

Returns:
    list: output times in ms

functions.name: bert_schemas.timing.decimal_times_are_unique
documentation:
Tests if all object times are in fact, unique

Args:
    ts (list): list of times

Returns:
    bool: true if times are unique

functions.name: bert_schemas.timing.decimal_to_float
documentation:
Converts decimal time to float

Args:
    d (Decimal): input time in ms
Returns:
    float: output time in ms, resolved to 0.1 resolution

functions.name: bert_schemas.timing.decimals_to_floats
documentation:
Converts lists of decimal times to floats

Args:
    ds (list): input times in ms

Returns:
    list: output times in ms, resolved to 0.1 resolution

functions.name: bert_schemas.timing.float_to_decimal
documentation:
Converts float to decimal value with 0.1 resolution

Args:
    ds (list): input times in ms

Returns:
    list: output times in ms, resolved to 0.1 resolution

functions.name: bert_schemas.timing.floats_to_decimals
documentation:
Converts lists of decimals to floats

Args:
    fs (list): input times in ms, as floats

Returns:
    list: output times in ms, resolved to 0.1 resolution

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.timing.Settings.max_evap_duration
documentation: maximum allowed rf evaporation duration

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.timing.Settings.max_exp_time
documentation: maximum allowed user experiment time

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.timing.Settings.min_tof
documentation: minimum allowed value for time-of-flight

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.timing.Settings.max_tof
documentation: maximum allowed value for time-of-flight

functions.name: bert_schemas.tests.test_job_schemas.test_pulse_cross_validate
documentation: need Noah input
def test_barrier_get_position_spectra(barrier_data):
barrier = job_schema.Barrier(**barrier_data)
spectra = barrier.get_position_spectra([2, 3], [6, 3])

functions.name: bert_schemas.tests.test_job_schemas.test_job_base_input_run_populate
documentation: need Noah input
def test_laser_get_intensity_waveform():
laser_data = {
"type": "TERMINATOR",
"position_um": 1.0,
"pulses": [
"intensities_mw_per_cm2": [2, 3, 4],
"detuning_mhz": 2,
"interpolation": "LINEAR",
laser = job_schema.Laser(**laser_data)
waveform = laser.get_intensity_waveform(
tstart_ms=1.0, tend_ms=3.0, sample_rate_hz=1.0

modules.name: bert_schemas.job
documentation: type: ignore

functions.name: bert_schemas.job.interpolation_to_kind
documentation:
Method to convert our InterpolationType to something scipy can understand

Args:
    interpolation (bert_schemas.job.InterpolationType): Primitive job interpolation type

Returns:
    str: A "kind" string to be used by scipy's interp1d

functions.name: bert_schemas.job.interpolate_1d
documentation:
Method to interpolate a 1D list of pairs [xs, ys] at the evaluation point x

Args:
    xs (list[float]): List of x values
    ys (list[float]): List of y values
    x (float): Desired x-coordinate to evaluate the resulting interpolation function
    interpolation (job_schema.InterpolationType, optional): Interpolation style

Returns:
    float: Interpolation function value at the specified x-coordinate

functions.name: bert_schemas.job.interpolate_1d_list
documentation:
Method to interpolate a 1d list of pairs [xs, ys] at the evaluation points given by x_values

Args:
    xs (list[float]): List of x values
    ys (list[float]): List of y values
    x_values (list[float]): Desired x-coordinates to evaluate the resulting interpolation function
    interpolation (job_schema.InterpolationType, optional): Interpolation style

Returns:
    list[float]: Floating point values corresponding to evaluation of the interpolation function
        value at the specified x_values

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.job.RfInterpolationType.PREVIOUS
documentation: assumes value of previous data point

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.job.InterpolationType.ZERO
documentation: spline interpolation at zeroth order

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.job.InterpolationType.SLINEAR
documentation: spline interpolation at first order

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.job.InterpolationType.QUADRATIC
documentation: spline interpolation at second order

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.job.InterpolationType.CUBIC
documentation: spline interpolation at third order

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.job.InterpolationType.NEAREST
documentation: assumes value of nearest data point

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.job.InterpolationType.PREVIOUS
documentation: assumes value of previous data point

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.job.InterpolationType.NEXT
documentation: assumes value of next data point

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.job.AbsorptionImages.atoms
documentation: Absolute Image

class_variables.name: bert_schemas.job.AbsorptionImages.back
documentation: Background Image

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Landscape.get_ideal_potential
documentation:
Method to get the ideal potential energy at the specified positions

Args:
    positions (list, optional): List of positions in microns

Returns:
    list[float]: Potential energies, in kHz, at the specified positions

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Landscape.get_potential
documentation:
Method to calculate the optical potential associated with a Landscape object,
taking into account the actual implementation of the Oqtant projection system,
at the given time

Args:
    positions (list[float]): Positions, in microns, where the potential should be evaluated

Returns:
    list[float]: Potential energies, in kHz, at the specified positions

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Landscape.get_position_spectrum
documentation: Get positional weights over the given positions

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.OpticalLandscape.get_ideal_potential
documentation:
Method to calculate ideal object potential energy at the specified time and positions

Args:
    time (float): Time, in ms, at which the potential energy is calculated
    positions (list[float], optional): Positions at which the potential energy is calculated

Returns:
    list[float]: Potential energies, in kHz, at specified time and positions

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.OpticalLandscape.get_potential
documentation:
Method to calculate the optical potential associated with a Landscape object,
taking into account the actual implementation of the Oqtant projection system,
at the given time

Args:
    time (float): Time, in ms, at which to sample the potential energy
    positions (list[float], optional): Positions, in microns, where the potential should be evaluated

Returns:
    list[float]: Potential energies, in kHz, at the requested positions and time

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.OpticalLandscape.get_position_spectra
documentation: Get the position spectrum over the specified positions at the given times

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.native_widths
documentation: data can be a Barrier object or a dict

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.lifetime
documentation:
Property to get the lifetime value of a Barrier object

Returns:
    float: The amount of time, in ms, that the barrier will exist

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.birth
documentation:
Property to get the (manipulation stage) time that the Barrier object will be created

Returns:
    float: The time, in ms, at which the barrier will start being projected

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.death
documentation:
Property to get the (manipulation stage) time that the Barrier object will cease to exist

Returns:
    float: The time, in ms, at which the barrier will stop being projected

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.is_precision
documentation:
Property to ask if this barrier is a "precision" one, i.e. one with the narrowest possible
width at all times and the smoothest possible dynamics.

Returns:
    bool: True if the Barrier object is classified as precision, False otherwise

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.evolve
documentation:
Method to evolve the position, height, and/or width of a Barrier object over a duration

Args:
    duration (TimeMs): The time, in ms, over which evolution should take place
    position (ProjectedPosition | None, optional): The position, in microns, to evolve to
    height (ProjectedEnergy | None, optional): The height, in kHz, to evolve to
    width (BarrierWidth | None, optional): The width, in microns, to evolve to

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.is_active
documentation:
Method to determine if a Barrier object is active (exists) at the specified time

Args:
    time (float): The time, in ms, at which the query is evaluated

Returns:
    bool: Flag indicating if the barrier exists or not at the specified time

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.get_positions
documentation:
Method to calculate the Barrier object position at the specified (manipulation stage) times

Args:
    times (list[float]): The times, in ms, at which positions are calculated

Returns:
    list[float]: The positions, in microns, at the specified times

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.get_position
documentation:
Method to calculate the Barrier object position at the specified (manipulation stage) time

Args:
    time (float): The time, in ms, at which the position is calculated

Returns:
    float: The position, in microns, at the specified time

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.get_heights
documentation:
Method to calculate the Barrier object heights at the specified list of times

Args:
    times (list[float]): The times, in ms, at which the heights are calculated

Returns:
    list[float]: The barrier heights at the specified times

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.get_height
documentation:
Method to get the Barrier object height at the specified time

Args:
    time (float): The time, in ms, at which the height is calculated

Returns:
    float: The barrier height at the specified time

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.get_widths
documentation:
Method to calculate the Barrier object widths at the specified times

Args:
    times (list[float]): The times, in ms, at which the heights are calculated

Returns:
    list[float]: The barrier widths at the specified times

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.get_width
documentation:
Method to calculate the Barrier object width at the specified time

Args:
    times (float): The time, in ms, at which the height is calculated

Returns:
    float: The barrier width at the specified time

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.get_ideal_potential
documentation:
Method to calculate the ideal Barrier object potential energy at the given positions
and at the specified time without taking into account finite projection system resolution
to update time of projected light

Args:
    time (float, optional): The time, in ms, at which the potential is calculated
    positions (list[float], optional): The positions, in microns, at which the potential
        energies are evaluated

Returns:
    list[float]: The potential energies, in kHz, at the specified positions

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.get_potential
documentation:
Method to calculate the optical potential associated with a Barrier object, taking into
account the actual implementation of the Oqtant projection system

Args:
    time (float): The time, in ms, at which the potential should be evaluated
    positions (list[float], optional): The positions, in microns, at which the potential should be evaluated

Returns:
    list[float]: The potential energies, in kHz, at the specified positions

methods.name: bert_schemas.job.Barrier.get_position_spectrum
documentation: Get positional weights over the given positions at the specified time

classes.name: bert_schemas.job.JobPost
documentation: needed for post fixtures
